prose:
Method provenance
Property: Frontier-orbital energies (HOMO, LUMO) and HOMO–LUMO gap of aniline; units eV.
Level of theory: DFT, tier "standard" = B3LYP/def2-TZVP, exact four-center integrals (density fitting OFF) in PySCF.
Geometry provenance: user-supplied /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/frontier-orbitals-validation/aniline/aniline.xyz; single-point only (no re-optimization).
Solvent / environment: gas phase (no implicit solvation model).
Charge & multiplicity: 0, 1 (closed-shell singlet).
Software: PySCF backend (version not reported by engine).
Convergence & caveats: SCF converged in 9 cycles; no warnings reported. integrity.trustworthy = true.  No density-fitting approximation was used.
Live log: the SCF/analysis output was streamed to the standard chemkit live “.out” file announced at job launch; result JSON written to /home/erobles/chem-skills/.fidelity_scratch/tmp1vkmo868/live.json.

Results (selected):
• HOMO energy = −5.553772 eV
• LUMO energy = −0.2489544 eV
→ HOMO–LUMO gap = 5.3048177 eV (Koopmans-type orbital gap, not an excitation energy)

No warnings were emitted by the engine.

The calculation was therefore completed successfully, and the reported gap of 5.3048177 eV is the headline value for the B3LYP/def2-TZVP (gas-phase) single-point on the supplied aniline geometry.
